current_activity: Discovery for the backend lane is kept to one read-only worker task that reconciles MVP backend scope, API boundary questions, persistence unknowns, and lane-local dependencies into a single discovery assertion without creating cross-lane handoffs.
Fast-path discovery plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.
Minimal frontend discovery lane plan with one read-only worker task to capture MVP UI boundaries, integration assumptions, and unresolved questions from the supplied planning inputs.
Fast-path discovery plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.
Plan a single middleware discovery task to reconcile the integration lane boundary, open contract questions, and external dependencies for the todo application objective.
Fast-path discovery plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Backend discovery scope reconciled from the injected planning inputs. MVP backend responsibility is limited to storing and serving todo items for a React frontend through a simple CRUD API with persistence across reloads. Backend-local design carry-forwards are the undecided API shape, canonical todo item schema and identifiers, persistence mechanism, and minimal reliability rules for update/delete behavior and completed-state changes. No cross-lane contradiction was supplied in the resolved inputs, so these remain backend-owned design decisions rather than discovery blockers.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Middleware discovery scope reconciled from injected planning inputs. The middleware lane is limited to frontend-backend contract mediation, orchestration handoffs, and defining the minimal end-to-end validation path for the MVP todo app. Frontend UI behavior, backend framework selection, and persistence implementation remain external producer responsibilities. Key integration questions to carry into design are the API contract shape the React client will consume, backend error/validation semantics, the persistence choice impact on CRUD behavior, and the exact MVP test depth needed to approve integrated delivery.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Reviewed the supplied planning inputs for the React todo frontend discovery lane. The MVP frontend scope is limited to listing todo items and allowing create, edit, complete/uncomplete, and delete interactions, while API shape, persistence behavior, and cross-layer integration contracts remain outside frontend authority. Validation completed.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.
Plan a single backend design task that produces the authoritative todo API contract and backend design artifact covering persistence, data model, and validation criteria for the MVP.
Fast-path design plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Single-task frontend design reconciliation plan that fixes the MVP React todo UI scope, interaction states, and consumer-side API assumptions from discovery inputs without redefining shared contracts.
Fast-path design plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Single-task design plan for the middleware lane: reconcile discovery findings into the authoritative middleware integration scope and minimal end-to-end validation criteria for downstream phases without expanding into frontend or backend implementation ownership.
Fast-path design plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Reconciled the frontend MVP design baseline from the injected discovery inputs into a consumer-side contract: a responsive single-user todo UI must support list rendering plus loading, empty, populated, and recoverable error states; item creation and editing use the same minimal field-level rule of required non-empty todo text; each listed item exposes complete or uncomplete and delete actions; and persistence across reloads is treated as an upstream API and storage dependency rather than a frontend-owned contract. Shared API shape details remain consumer constraints only.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Middleware design scope was reconciled into one MVP integration assertion using the injected discovery inputs and the discovery lane report. The approved boundary is a single frontend-to-backend todo workflow with stable CRUD contract behavior and persistence across reloads; backend framework and storage selection remain backend-owned implementation choices, optional features such as authentication are excluded from MVP scope, and the minimal downstream validation path is publication of the shared integration contract plus an integrated smoke flow covering create, edit, complete, uncomplete, delete, and persisted reload behavior.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Authored the backend design package for the todo MVP: a REST JSON CRUD contract for `/api/todos` and a backend design spec selecting SQLite-backed persistence, component boundaries, read/write flows, error semantics, and backend-scoped validation criteria.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Implement the React todo MVP against the approved backend contract with a dedicated API client, a single App shell, and a final task that adds behavior coverage and emits the acceptance review bundle.
Lean MVP build plan with one frontend delivery lane that consumes the approved todo API and integration scope, implements the React todo workflow, and hands a concrete review bundle to acceptance.

Plan the middleware MVP-build lane as a small two-step integration package: first materialize the middleware-owned runtime wireup proof from approved frontend/backend design inputs, then emit the final integration contract and acceptance-ready build report bundle from that proof.
Lean MVP-build plan with one middleware lane that authors the shared integration contract and emits the concrete acceptance bundle for the integrated todo workflow.
Codex turn completed.
